text.title: Reporting Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Reporting Basics methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: KPIs MUST be actionable (lead-time, cycle-time, throughput, WIP, NPS); vanity metrics (story points done, hours logged) are rejected.

rationale.source: Reporting practice (Hubbard, How to Measure Anything 2014)

rule.rationale: vanity metrics drive ceremony, not decisions.

rule.statement: Every report MUST carry a 3-5 line narrative summary at the top; bare numbers without narrative are rejected.
rule.rationale: numbers without narrative are noise; narrative anchors stakeholder attention.

rule.statement: Any KPI breaching threshold MUST emit an explicit escalation_flag with owner + proposed action; silent breaches are rejected.
rule.rationale: silent breaches train stakeholders to stop reading the report.

rule.statement: Distribution list MUST be named recipients (not generic group aliases); generic-only distribution is rejected.
rule.rationale: generic aliases drop reports into the void.

rule.statement: Cadence (weekly Mon 10:00, monthly first business day) MUST be locked; drift requires explicit ADR.
rule.rationale: drifting cadence kills the muscle memory.

rule.statement: Every KPI MUST cite its data source (dashboard URL, query ID, raw export); un-traceable numbers are rejected.
rule.rationale: agent-supplied numbers without source are fabricated by default.
